event.summary: Recorded passing validation; acceptance remains a separate explicit transition.

acceptance.proof: A public bounded XmlElementTree accepts caller-ordered candidate nodes containing only accepted XmlElementName containers or accepted XmlLeafElement leaves. Index zero is the sole root, every later parent is strictly earlier, and leaves cannot parent children. Empty arenas, root parents, later roots, out-of-range/self/forward parents, and leaf parents reject with typed indexed errors.

Four caller-selected nonzero maxima bound total nodes, root-one depth, direct children per container, and total retained UTF-8 bytes across owned element names and represented character data. Exact limits succeed; one-past limits, checked retained-byte overflow, and malformed maxima return stable typed payloads. Retained bytes are an arena resource bound, never a serialized or wire-size claim.

Validation is iterative and nonrecursive with fixed precedence: empty arena, node bound, root-parent shape, one fallible scratch reservation, each later node in caller order for parent identity, parent container kind, depth, and direct-child bound, then checked retained-byte calculation and retained-byte bound. Scratch allocation failure is typed and non-panicking.

Accepted private state contains only validated limits and the original ordered candidate-node Vec moved without a second arena allocation. Borrowed inspection and consuming recovery preserve parent indexes, container/leaf classification, component limits, exact strings, represented character data, node-vector allocation, and component string allocations. Owning node and tree types expose neither Clone nor mutable access.

A separate LSLC-001E local-results overlay binds independently authored tests only to accepted LSLC-001B through LSLC-001D component contracts and structural corpus roles. Every LSLC-001A oracle and candidate role remains not-observed with null evidence, and the hierarchy assigns no info, desc, stream-info, serialization, or official-liblsl meaning.

The crate remains dependency-, feature-, unsafe-, I/O-, activation-, parser-, serializer-, protocol-, wire-, runtime-, FFI-, adapter-, provider-, and device-free; historical evidence remains unchanged and the feature/effect lock remains empty and inert.

document.commit_policy: Commit the accepted container/leaf hierarchy separately from MetadataTree conversion, stream-info projection, tag spelling, document assembly, serialization, oracle capture, protocol, transport, adapters, providers, or runtime work.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the bounded container/leaf hierarchy, fallible scratch validation, and strict no-document/no-LSL-mapping boundary.
Describe the actual hierarchical capability and resource bounds without presenting caller order as serialization order or the model as a complete XML tree/document.
Review whether fallible scratch validation and retained-resource bounds add reusable guidance; retain the shared skill unchanged if its existing bounded-state and evidence rules are sufficient.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review the clean-source, public-evidence, and future MetadataTree projection boundary; retain the shared router unchanged if existing guidance covers the unit.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: MetadataTree conversion, StreamDefinition or StreamDescriptor projection, info/desc/core/runtime/channel-metadata roles, stream-info meaning, or any LSL field mapping.

Mixed content, ordered text/child interleaving, values attached to containers, children attached to leaves, attributes, namespaces, declarations, comments, processing instructions, CDATA sections, schemas, queries, or general XML DOM behavior.

Document production, well-formed-document claims, start/end/empty-tag spelling, caller-order serialization meaning, whitespace layout, parsing, decoding, mutation, serialization, canonicalization, raw bytes, numeric spelling, channel-format spelling, or endpoint representation.

Oracle capture, candidate differential comparison, official-liblsl behavior or compatibility claims, protocol versions, wire framing, discovery, resolution, networking, sockets, inlets, outlets, transport, buffering, queues, threads, clocks, recovery, providers, FFI, native loading, adapters, or language bindings.

Cargo dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, additional crates, generated protocol material, copied or translated liblsl or rLSL source, production liblsl integration, device validation, or Morphospace runtime authority.
document.objective: Implement a dependency-free, explicitly bounded, parent-before-child container/leaf XML element hierarchy over accepted LSLC-001B through LSLC-001D components, with iterative fallible validation, exact arena ownership, and no complete-document, MetadataTree-conversion, stream-info-mapping, serialization, protocol, wire, or runtime behavior.
outputs: Dependency-free XmlElementTreeLimits, candidate container/leaf node, accepted XmlElementTree, and typed XmlElementTreeError APIs with deterministic iterative structural, resource, arithmetic, and scratch-allocation validation.

Positive and damaged tests proving root/parent/container structure, exact and one-past bounds, precedence, non-panicking scratch allocation failure, caller-order and allocation preservation, and absence of Clone or mutable access.

A separate LSLC-001E local-results overlay, deterministic focused checker, aggregate-gate routing, and public documentation that preserves every oracle, serialization, LSL-mapping, and authority boundary.
